title: alnus glutinosa f. graeca
intro: Alnus glutinosa f. graeca, also known as the Greek alder, is a subspecies of the black alder tree, native to the Balkan Peninsula. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and skin problems, thanks to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Alnus glutinosa f. graeca, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various ailments.
